commit.message:
fix: unblock signals — cooldown gate, silent rate-limits, Bot() per message, import overhead (#81)

* Initial plan

* fix: comprehensive signal-blocking gates, logging, and performance improvements

- CRITICAL: Cooldown gate no longer blocks CH3 (Easy Breakout); signal lifecycle
  tracking (TP/SL) runs independently of cooldown; only CH1/CH2 are gated
- CRITICAL: Log WARNING (not DEBUG) when `can_open_signal()` blocks a signal,
  including symbol, side, and max cap count for visibility
- CRITICAL: Log WARNING (not DEBUG) when a signal is rate-limited by
  MIN_SIGNAL_GAP_SECONDS for CH1, CH2, and CH3
- MEDIUM: Move hot-path in-function imports to module level (hard_scalp,
  medium_scalp, easy_breakout, funding_rate, session_filter, correlation_guard)
- MEDIUM: Use shared `_application.bot` in `_broadcast_to_channel` to avoid
  creating a new HTTP session per Telegram message; falls back to fresh Bot
  context when _application is not yet initialised
- MEDIUM: Remove redundant nested async Bot() closures for correlation alerts
  and latency alerts — use _broadcast_to_channel instead
- MEDIUM: Create a single `ThreadPoolExecutor` across all seed batches instead
  of creating/destroying one per batch (both futures and spot seeding)
- LOW: Fix `_check_weight()` ordering in exchange.py — threshold is checked
  BEFORE incrementing so the logged counter is accurate and no over-counting
  occurs when the window boundary is crossed
- LOW: Add `_application` module-level reference, set in `build_application()`
- LOW: Document spot WebSocket `on_candle_close=None` as intentional
- LOW: Reduce `MIN_SIGNAL_GAP_SECONDS` default from 300s (5 min) to 60s in
  both pydantic settings model and env-var fallback in config.py

All 1167 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: kishore446 <78694720+kishore446@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix agent prompt: retry on invalid input, resolve target before manifest write

promptAgentChoice now retries up to 3 times on invalid input instead of
failing immediately. This prevents transient empty-input issues from
aborting the install flow.

Move resolveInstallTargets() (which may prompt for agent choice) before
manifest/pinfile writes in both get.go and install.go. Previously, a
failed prompt left dependencies persisted in the manifest, causing
confusing retry behavior where the user's answer was parsed as a URL
argument.

Add 6 tests for promptAgentChoice covering: valid input, Both selection,
retry on empty, retry on garbage, exhausted retries, and EOF.

commit.message: Bug fixes for running a large/complicated solution on AL-Go (#2075) ### ❔What, Why & How This PR fixes a few minor bugs and 3 major, all changes required for moving Bunker Holding Group to AL-Go for GitHub [Bug] Fix a small spelling mistake in DownloadProjectDependencies.Action.ps1‎ [Bug] Avoid failing entire workflows due to cleanup issues (f.ex. if no Container is used) in PipelineCleanup.ps1 Fixes #2086 - In AL-Go-Helper.ps1 The sort order is changed from this: to this: Which completes in 2:01, saving 45 minutes from the first run, just by re-ordering and allowing people to see compile errors up-front - fail early! The key difference here is just the order in which projects are built/tested. In the first run, build3 had to wait 30 minutes until the test run was done before building the next jobs, and 1 hour and 44 until the last build jobs would run. In the second run - all test jobs are running in parallel at the very end, getting compile errors quicly and postponing test runs until the very last. Subsequently, when using GitHub hosted Custom runners, we can achieve this: Note that the custom runners are running tests in 40 minutes, where the GitHub hosted runners takes 104 minutes. This is because the GitHub hosted custom runners are using SQL Server Developer edition and no docker. Saving a full 2 hours, bringing a complete build from 2 hours 46 minutes down to 46 minutes. Fixes #2085 - In GetDependencies in GitHub-Helper.ps1 When downloading artifacts from projects build earlier in the same workflow, it flags projects as missing if either Apps or TestApps are missing. This means that projects, which only contains TestApps or Apps will be downloaded but also looked for in the last known good build later on. It doesn't fail, because it cannot find the artifacts in that built either. See https://github.com/Freddy-DK/DependenciesProblems/actions/runs/21079606240/job/60630194330 Fixes #2084 in GetArtifactsFromWorkflowRun in GitHub-Helper.ps1 If a build is cancelled during tests or failing due to instability - and you subsequently re-run failed jobs (not all jobs) you will get multiple artifacts for succeeding projects and subsequent jobs cannot locate these artifacts (they get an array of artifacts). The "double" artifacts are not visible in the UI, but found when artifacts are queried. You could see this as a bug in GitHub, but we should fix this in AL-Go in a way that works, even if GitHub fixes their bug. This build: https://github.com/Freddy-DK/DependenciesProblems/actions/runs/21090442931 Has double artifacts: https://api.github.com/repos/Freddy-DK/DependenciesProblems/actions/runs/21090442931/artifacts When than downloading artifacts from prior builds for incremental builds, it fails with ``` throw "Multiple artifacts found with mask $artifactMask for project $projectName" ``` See: https://github.com/Freddy-DK/DependenciesProblems/actions/runs/21090506962/job/60661024240 ### ✅ Checklist - [x] Add tests (E2E, unit tests) - [x] Update RELEASENOTES.md - [ ] Update documentation (e.g. for new settings or scenarios) - [ ] Add telemetry --------- Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com> Co-authored-by: Maria Zhelezova <43066499+mazhelez@users.noreply.github.com> Co-authored-by: Alexander Holstrup <117829001+aholstrup1@users.noreply.github.com>

skillpm. This PR clarifies that those specs are host/runtime extension
mechanisms, while skillpm remains focused on portable Agent Skills
packaging and orchestration.

- Plugin specs define host-specific extension/runtime hooks.
- Agent Skills define portable instruction packages that can be reused across agent systems.

For skillpm, the model stays the same: npm for dependency management, plus `skills` and `add-mcp` for wiring and MCP configuration.

fix: correctly detect untranslated PO entries with multi-line msgstr (#57)

The `i18n-issues` workflow was keeping issue #29 open despite all Dutch
strings being translated. The awk script matched `msgstr ""$` as
untranslated without checking for continuation lines — so any long
translation wrapped across lines was incorrectly flagged as missing.

**Root cause**

PO multi-line translations use `msgstr ""` as an opener, with the actual
content on subsequent `"..."` lines:

```po
msgid ""
"Long source string that wraps "
"to the next line."
msgstr ""
"Long translated string that "
"wraps to the next line."   ← was ignored; entry flagged as untranslated
```

**Fix — `.github/workflows/i18n-issues.yml`**

- Replaced the single-line `msgstr ""$` pattern with full msgstr state
tracking, mirroring the existing msgid multi-line handling
- The script now assembles the complete msgstr value across continuation
lines and only emits an untranslated entry when the final assembled
value is `""`
- Added an `END` rule to handle the last entry in a file with no
trailing blank line

Co-authored-by: jeffredodd <2861516+jeffredodd@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: eliminate layout-thrashing padding-left animation from sidebar toggle

The sidebar-transition CSS was animating padding-left on <main> alongside
the sidebar width, forcing a full layout-recalc + repaint on every frame
(~15 frames at 60fps over 250ms). Since the sidebar is position:fixed its
width change never affects main's document flow — only the padding-left
transition causes reflow, stressing 132 tiles × 5 shadow layers on the
home page with each frame.

- Remove padding-left (and padding-top) from .sidebar-transition so only
  the sidebar's own width animates; main content padding snaps in one frame
- Drop padding-left from will-change on .is-transitioning accordingly
- Add contain: layout style paint to the board frame wrapper in
  ActivePageDisplay, isolating the tile grid from the single-frame padding
  snap so tiles don't need to repaint when the sidebar toggles

* feat: add 3D split-flap tile animation to BoardDisplay

Replace the instant character-swap animation with a true split-flap
effect using CSS 3D transforms. Each tile now uses a 4-layer structure
during animation: the old character's top half folds down past the
midpoint (flapDown, ease-in) while the new character's bottom half
unfolds into place (flapUp, settling cubic-bezier), matching the
mechanics of a real Solari board. Flip cadence set to 80ms per
character to match Vestaboard "Fast" mode.

Add SplitFlapAnimation Storybook story for visual verification.

Replaces the inline badge stats in the PageHeader children slot with a
